validation.unsupportedFormat: Unsupported audio format. Supported formats: WAV, MP3, M4A
validation.fileTooLarge: The file is too large. Maximum size is 2GB

errors.onlyOneFileAtATime: A file is currently being analyzed. Please remove the current file before uploading another.

home.title: Quick Application Guide
home.description: Start by uploading evidence and progress through metadata, patterns, and results analysis to complete a consistent forensic evaluation.
home.intro: Auditra is an audio forensic analysis application designed to detect whether an audio file was generated by artificial intelligence. Follow this quick guide to understand how to use each module and obtain results.
home.noticeText: To better understand the analysis process, follow this order: Upload Audio -> Metadata -> Patterns -> Results Analysis.

upload.title: Upload the evidence
upload.description: Upload a single file (WAV, MP3, or M4A). Auditra does not alter the original file; it always works on a copy to ensure evidence integrity.

metadata.title: Review metadata
metadata.description: Inspect all types of metadata to detect traces left by AI. Auditra uses standard forensic tools such as Mutagen, Wave, ExifTool, and FFprobe to extract information.

patterns.title: Analyze binary patterns
patterns.description: Visually explore byte-level self-similarity and edge padding to identify synthesis fingerprints.

analysis.title: Get the final conclusion
analysis.description: Based on the results from the previous analysis steps, Auditra generates a final assessment on possible AI generation.

audioIngestion.title: Upload Audio Evidence
audioIngestion.description: Securely upload and catalog audio evidence.
audioIngestion.dragDrop: Drag and drop audio files here
audioIngestion.uploadInfo: You will be able to analyze the file to detect whether it contains AI-generated audio. You can only upload one file at a time. Once uploaded, you can remove it to upload another. All evidence analysis is performed on a copy of the original evidence, which remains unaltered to guarantee chain of custody integrity.
audioIngestion.highNoiseText: Visualizes self-similarity patterns in the byte stream and detects padding sequences at initial and final offsets, revealing the digital fingerprint of synthesis engines.
audioIngestion.batchText: Inspects all metadata of the uploaded file. Looks for traces that AI may have left behind, such as encoding libraries, timestamp inconsistencies, unusual codecs, or anomalies in audio configuration.

metadata.title: Metadata Analysis
metadata.description: This module extracts the complete technical information from your file using advanced forensic tools and libraries such as Mutagen, Wave, ExifTool, and FFprobe. View all extracted metadata to identify traces of manipulation, specific software, inconsistencies, and anomalies in the file metadata that may indicate AI generation.
metadata.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'Upload Audio' section first.

patterns.title: Pattern Analysis
patterns.description: Visualize byte-level self-similarity and file-edge padding to detect possible patterns left by AI. Self-similarity reveals repetition patterns in the data, while padding analysis detects homogeneous sequences that are indicative of automated processing.
patterns.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'Upload Audio' section first.
patterns.resolutionDescription: Press the buttons below to change the resolution and automatically recalculate the visualization
patterns.invalidWidth: Width must be between 128 and 2048 bytes
patterns.autosimilarityDesc: Each pixel represents the value of one byte in the file: dark colors are low values (0x00) and light colors are high values (0xFF). Self-similarity reveals repetition patterns in the data. Natural audio shows gradual and varied transitions, while AI-generated synthetic audio tends to show more regular, repetitive patterns or uniform blocks that reveal its artificial processing.
patterns.whatToLookForIntro: Natural audio is chaotic and diffuse, it does not exhibit regular patterns. AI-generated audio could be geometric, symmetric, and predictable.
whatToLookForItems: Columns and rows (Straight lines): Specific byte blocks that repeat identically at different scales.
Grid pattern: Mathematically exact time division indicating that AI processes in fixed blocks (e.g: 20ms), leaving a cyclic fingerprint that doesn't exist in human speech.
patterns.paddingDesc: Examines the first and last 1024 bytes of the file. In AI-generated audio, it is common to find sequences of repetitive bytes (padding), indicating automated processing. Natural audio typically does not exhibit this type of homogeneous pattern.

analysis.title: AI Detection Analysis
analysis.description: Analysis that integrates metadata and patterns to determine whether the audio was generated by artificial intelligence. Uses 10 indicators based on encoding fingerprints, audio configuration, codecs, file format, size, and padding patterns.
analysis.noFileMessage: Upload a file in the 'Upload Audio' section first.

encoding_library.reasoning: Libraries such as FFmpeg/Lavf are common in audio synthesis tools. The metadata tags shown below indicate where encoding libraries were detected. Look for these tags in the Metadata section to verify the detected values.

mono_audio.reasoning: Mono audio is less common in natural recordings and frequent in AI synthesis

details.channels_explanation: Natural audio recordings typically have 2 channels (stereo) or more. AI-generated audio frequently uses mono (single channel), which is less common in real recordings.

padding_pattern.reasoning: An anomalous amount of exact byte sequences has been detected. This structural homogeneity is a strong indicator of AI algorithms that pad data blocks to meet predefined sizes or to introduce silences.

details.top_patterns_explanation: Shows the byte values (in hexadecimal) that form the largest repetitive blocks in the file.
details.max_sequence_explanation: The longest uninterrupted sequence of the exact same byte.

timestamp_consistency.reasoning_identical: Creation and modification timestamps are identical
timestamp_consistency.reasoning_reversed: Creation timestamp is later than modification

details.relationship_normal_explanation: The file was created and then modified afterward, which is the expected behavior for legitimate files.
details.relationship_identical_explanation: The creation and modification times are exactly the same. This is suspicious because files should have different timestamps when created vs. modified. AI tools sometimes create files with identical timestamps.
details.relationship_creation_after_modification: The creation timestamp is later than the modification timestamp, which is logically impossible. This indicates that the file's metadata has been tampered with or artificially created.

codec_consistency.reasoning_mismatch: Codec inconsistent with format may indicate manipulation or re-encoding
codec_consistency.reasoning_error: Error: format or codec not available for verification

details.codec_explanation: The codec is the method used to compress/encode audio data. Each file format expects specific codecs. An inconsistency indicates the file may have been converted or manipulated.
details.expected_explanation: These are the standard codecs that should be used with this file format.

file_size.reasoning_ok: File size consistent with bitrate and duration
file_size.reasoning_high_deviation: Size deviated >30% from expected - HIGH risk
file_size.reasoning_medium_deviation: Size deviated 20-30% from expected - MEDIUM risk
file_size.reasoning_low_deviation: Size deviated <20% from expected - LOW risk

details.deviation_low_explanation: The file size differs by {{deviation}}% from what we would expect based on the bitrate and duration. This small deviation is normal and within acceptable ranges.
details.deviation_medium_explanation: The file size differs by {{deviation}}% from the expected size. This suggests the file may have been re-encoded or compressed, which can occur with AI processing.
details.deviation_high_explanation: The file size differs significantly ({{deviation}}%) from what it should mathematically be based on bitrate and duration. This is a strong indicator of re-encoding, additional processing, or manipulation.
details.size_explanation: The file is larger or smaller than it mathematically should be. Files that have been modified or synthesized often have size mismatches.
details.specs_explanation: Shows the audio duration and bitrate (quality). These two values determine what the file size should be.
details.compression_explanation: {{type}} compression is how audio data is stored. Lossy formats allow more size variation due to quality adjustments.

self_similarity.reasoning: Byte-level self-similarity has been detected at different scales. Go to the Patterns section to view the byte-level repetition visualization.

sample_rate_anomaly.reasoning: A sample rate of 16kHz, 22.05kHz, 24kHz, 32kHz, or 40kHz is typical of AI-generated audio. Natural recordings typically use 44.1kHz or 48kHz.

details.sample_rate_explanation: The number of audio samples per second.
details.typical_ai_rates_explanation: AI models typically generate audio at these sample rates.

atypical_bitrate.reasoning: An abnormally low bitrate was detected. AI models often use very low bitrates (24-48 kbps for lossy formats, <300 kbps for lossless formats) to reduce resource usage.

details.format_type_explanation: Lossy formats (MP3, M4A) compress audio with quality loss. Lossless formats (WAV) preserve all data.
details.bitrate_kbps_explanation: The number of kilobits per second. Lower values indicate more compression or reduced quality.
details.threshold_kbps_explanation: Bitrates below this threshold are suspicious for AI-generated audio.

precise_duration.reasoning: The audio duration is mathematically exact (e.g., 15.000s or 30.500s). Human recordings have irregular durations; AI-generated audio often has exact boundaries.

details.exact_duration_explanation: The total duration of the audio file in seconds.
details.decimal_part_explanation: The fractional part of the duration in seconds.

conclusions.no_indicators: No indicators of AI generation were detected. This audio appears to be an authentic recording.
conclusions.low_risk: The analysis indicates a low risk of AI generation. There is no conclusive evidence of artificial processing.
conclusions.medium_risk: The analysis indicates a medium risk of AI generation. The file is suspicious, and a more in-depth analysis is recommended.
conclusions.high_risk_critical: The analysis indicates a high risk of AI generation. Critical indicators were detected. This audio was most likely generated by synthesis tools or AI.
conclusions.high_risk_multiple: The analysis indicates a high risk of AI generation. Multiple indicators suggest artificial processing.

recommendations.padding_pattern: An artificial hexadecimal padding pattern has been detected. It is recommended to inspect the binary structure to rule out the use of algorithmic generators, which complete data blocks in a predictable manner, unlike natural encoding.
recommendations.timestamp_consistency: Chronological anomaly detected. It is recommended to treat the evidence as manipulated; the file has been re-encoded or altered after its purported original capture date.
recommendations.encoding_library: Presence of software encoding libraries (e.g., FFmpeg/Lavf) without traces of physical capture hardware. It is suggested to cross-reference this data with the declared source device in the investigation.
recommendations.mono_audio: Mono channel configuration detected. Since current mobile devices capture in stereo by default, this structural feature is highly compatible with Text-to-Speech (TTS) engines and automated processing. It is suggested to cross-reference this data with the declared source device in the investigation.
recommendations.codec_consistency: Discrepancy identified between the file container and the actual audio stream encoding. This behavior is a strong indicator of forced re-encoding.
recommendations.file_size: Mathematical inconsistency between the duration, bitrate, and total file size on disk. This anomaly suggests post-processing alteration, non-native compression, or metadata injection.
recommendations.self_similarity: The self-similarity matrix reveals high redundancy at the byte level. Maximum caution is required, as the lack of entropy and natural variability is characteristic of vocoders and generative neural networks.
recommendations.multiple_indicators: The convergence of multiple structural and metadata anomalies elevates the risk level to a critical state. It is recommended to discard the legitimacy of the evidence or refer it for in-depth forensic analysis.
recommendations.no_indicators: No structural forensic markers associated with synthetic generation have been detected. However, due diligence is advised, as advanced generative architectures can evade static detection.
recommendations.sample_rate_anomaly: Atypical sample rate for consumer hardware recording standards (e.g., 44.1kHz or 48kHz). This metric often coincides with the native output limitations of generative AI models.
recommendations.atypical_bitrate: Anomalous or excessively constant bitrate. This pattern is common in data streams processed to reduce computational cost.
recommendations.precise_duration: The temporal crop of the file is mathematically exact, which is unusual in human recordings due to the unpredictable nature of the capture. This feature is common in algorithmically generated or processed files.
